test_cases.title: Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA)
test_cases.description: Try to access an object you don't own by changing the ID in the URL (e.g., `/api/users/123` to `/api/users/456`).

test_cases.title: JWT Signature Bypass
test_cases.description: Change the JWT algorithm to `none` and remove the signature.

test_cases.title: API endpoint discovery and documentation drift
test_cases.description: Compare live endpoints against documentation/OpenAPI specs to uncover shadow APIs and deprecated methods.
steps: Gather any OpenAPI/Swagger definitions and import into Burp or Postman.
Run Kiterunner/ffuf wordlists against `/-/`, `/v1/`, `/beta/` to find undocumented paths or methods.
Diff responses and authentication requirements to identify drift from the published contract.
Recommend aligning documentation with deployment and disabling legacy routes.

test_cases.title: Mass assignment / JSON parameter tampering
test_cases.description: Probe JSON bodies for writable fields that should remain server-controlled (roles, pricing, flags).
steps: Send these properties back in create/update requests to test if the server honors them.
Observe for privilege escalation or business-logic abuse.
Recommend whitelisting allowed properties and using DTO validation.

test_cases.title: API rate limiting and replay gaps
test_cases.description: Evaluate throttling, burst handling, and nonce requirements for sensitive endpoints (login, password reset, financial ops).
steps: Send rapid bursts of requests with varying identifiers to detect per-IP vs per-account enforcement.
Replay signed requests or OTP submissions to verify nonce enforcement.
Note any 429 bypass via headers (X-Forwarded-For) or HTTP/2 multiplexing.
Recommend consistent throttling and cryptographic nonces.

test_cases.title: Server-side request forgery in API integrations
test_cases.description: Inspect webhook, URL preview, and import features for SSRF into cloud metadata/management endpoints.
steps: Identify parameters accepting URLs (webhooks, importers, screenshot APIs).
Send payloads targeting cloud metadata services (`http://169.254.169.254/`) or internal admin consoles.
Monitor for timeouts, DNS callbacks, or leaked data in responses/logs.
Recommend allowlists and layered egress controls.
